item.title: Move advisory complexity hints out of check_readiness_gates
item.intent: check_readiness_gates is a pure gate function — it checks hard requirements only. Advisory hints (under-specified warnings) live in the caller. Saves ~10 lines from the gate function and improves separation of concerns.
item.description: check_readiness_gates (98 lines) has 6 hard gate checks plus a 12-line advisory block (lines 648-660) that warns about under-specified items (few steps, no scope). The advisory block is not a gate — it never blocks anything. Move it to cmd_backlog_check (the primary caller) and the advisory check in cmd_run. This makes check_readiness_gates a clean pass/fail function.
steps: Extract the complexity hints block (step count < 3 advisory, no scope advisory) from check_readiness_gates
Add the advisory checks to cmd_backlog_check after the gate check
Update the advisory check in cmd_run dry-run and sprint loop to call the extracted logic
Verify: backlog check still shows advisory warnings alongside gate failures
acceptanceCriteria: check_readiness_gates contains only hard gate checks (title, intent, steps, AC, short steps, deps)
check_readiness_gates does not contain complexity_hints or under-specified logic
backlog check output still includes advisory warnings when relevant
